command_transcript:
Command chat:
  new [prompt...]
  review
  linearize
  quit

Patches and file locks are triggered automatically when agents interact with the orchestrator.

work-leaf> new add vim like visual mode for both panes when I do v I can select the text in focused the panes same keystrokes of vim y Y for copy maiusc V line select block select with ctrl v block select

work-leaf> new implement strict selected-agent slash command execution. When a selected agent chat message starts with / followed by a non-whitespace command token, Work Leaf must treat it as a backend command for that selected agent, execute it immediately, and append the backend command output in the chat. This is not the existing raw pass-through behavior: passing /status to the normal agent send/resume/model prompt path is insufficient and must be covered by a failing test. Add coverage for /status and /fork, including a test that proves the normal backend send path does not receive /status as an ordinary prompt.
work-leaf> new when review process is done the patch agent chat must be highlighted and ask is this feature done with yes/no; yes closes it, typing again reopens it
user-3 reviewed by review-user-3: rounds=2 resolved=yes
user-2 reviewed by review-user-2: rounds=3 resolved=yes
user-1 reviewed by review-user-1: rounds=3 resolved=yes

sessions.title: review user-agent
lines: codex: Codex session 019eb60e-bd7a-78a2-a7df-86dd2380c5a0
codex: Codex is working
@work-leaf read docs/architecture.md src/agent_runtime.rs src/cli.rs src/codex.rs src/workspace.rs tests/workspace.rs
I’m reading the orchestrator-provided bundle, then I’ll check the changed behavior against the architecture and tests before returning only the review verdict.

The bundle confirms this is an agent-facing change: `WorkLeafController::send_message` and `CommandChat` are in the path from selected terminal chat input to provider backend execution, so real-agent verification is in scope.

I’m checking for behavior bugs in the command path itself, not just the stated verification gap. The key question is whether `/status` reaches the same Codex session safely and preserves lifecycle controls.

I found one concrete behavioral risk beyond verification: the new Codex slash-command process is launched without being associated with the target agent id, which matters for per-agent interrupt handling. I’m pinning that to exact file lines from the bundle now.

The review evidence is sufficient: the patch path is clear from `workspace.rs` to `cli.rs` to `codex.rs`, and the launch/interrupt tracking mismatch is directly visible in the new command implementation.

FINDINGS

- `src/codex.rs:465` launches slash-command resumes with `agent_id: None`, so the process is not recorded in `active_processes` by `run_invocation_streaming` (`src/codex.rs:282-287`). The selected-agent path is `WorkLeafController::send_message` (`src/workspace.rs:259-275`) -> `CommandChat::run_agent_backend_command_streaming_with_ids` (`src/cli.rs:479-490`) -> `CodexBackend::run_command_streaming`; `CodexBackend::interrupt` only terminates PIDs found in `active_processes` (`src/codex.rs:399-409`). Result: a long-running or stuck `/status`/`/fork` command cannot be interrupted per-agent even though the UI reports Ctrl-C was sent. Pass `Some(agent_id)` into `run_invocation_streaming` for command execution and add coverage for interrupting an in-flight backend command.

- Required real-agent verification is missing for this agent-facing behavior. The patch changes selected-agent slash command routing and Codex resume invocation behavior (`src/workspace.rs:259-290`, `src/cli.rs:450-493`, `src/codex.rs:458-497`), but the review scope says: “No real-agent smoke scenario was run, and no exact local blocker was captured.” Evidence that would resolve this is an exact real Codex scenario, for example launching a real user agent, sending `/status` through selected-agent chat, and reporting the visible result, or the exact pre-agent initialization/auth blocker plus the automated tests that cover the behavior.

FINDINGS

- Required real-agent verification is still missing. The follow-up fixed the interrupt registration bug and ran `cargo test codex::tests::backend_command_can_be_interrupted_for_target_agent --lib`, but it still only verifies a fake Codex script. This patch changes selected-agent slash command routing into real Codex resume execution (`src/workspace.rs` -> `src/cli.rs` -> `src/codex.rs`), so the ready evidence still needs either an exact real Codex `/status` or `/fork` selected-agent scenario with the visible result, or an exact pre-agent blocker such as Codex initialization/auth failure plus the automated coverage that stands in for it.
NO_FINDINGS

codex: Codex is working
@work-leaf read docs/architecture.md src/ui.rs src/ui_harness.rs tests/ui_harness.rs tests/terminal_pty.rs src/workspace.rs src/lib.rs
I’ll use the orchestrator-provided bundle as the source snapshot, then send focused `@work-leaf` reads/edits for any project changes rather than touching files directly.

I have the architecture context and the UI surface is the likely ownership area: `TerminalUi`/`UiHarness` are public terminal frontend APIs, while controller/workspace state stays UI-neutral. I’m narrowing this to terminal rendering/input plus harness coverage.

The current terminal stack already centralizes modal key handling in `src/ui.rs::TerminalUi::handle_key_inner`, with tests driving it through `src/ui_harness.rs::UiHarness`. I’m reading those paths in detail so the patch stays inside the terminal layer and does not alter the controller API.
@work-leaf read src/terminal_app.rs src/bin/work-leaf.rs Cargo.toml

I have the second bundle with the runtime terminal adapter and Cargo metadata. I’m checking whether live copy can use an existing terminal clipboard path or needs to stay as a UI action routed by the adapter.

The binary has no clipboard dependency and the terminal app routes all printable bytes through `TerminalAppInput` into `TerminalUi`. I’m going to model visual mode as terminal UI state plus a copy action, then let the harness and terminal app record the copied text so behavior is testable without a platform clipboard dependency.
@work-leaf read src/cli.rs src/http_controller.rs src/bin/work-leaf.rs src/terminal_app.rs

I’m reading the CLI/controller transport only for the action text path; this determines whether visual-mode copy can be represented as a terminal-local action without changing agent/workspace behavior.

The copy feedback is currently a transcript/status-style concern: `src/cli.rs::ui_action_text` maps `UiAction` into visible text and `src/terminal_app.rs::TerminalAppCore::record_actions` records it. I’ll keep that pattern so no agent/backend or HTTP API has to know about text selection.

For the feature itself, the clean shape is: extend `UiMode` with visual variants, track a selection range over the focused pane’s rendered text, support `v`, `V`, and `Ctrl-V`, and emit one `CopySelection` action for `y`/`Y`. The harness can assert the copied string from pane text, which satisfies the UI test requirement without involving a system clipboard.

I’m going to submit this as one cohesive patch with tests and implementation together. Splitting a red test-only patch would violate the shared worktree rule, so the focused validation will be run after the cohesive edit lands.

The edit failed before applying because one `src/ui.rs` hunk was too broad, and another agent has added `TerminalUi::set_agent_hidden_state` near the same area. I’m rebasing the same terminal-only change against that compact refresh and tightening the contexts so the orchestrator can apply it deterministically.

Patch scope for `user-1` commit `651339a7`:

Implemented terminal-local Vim-style visual selection across both panes.

Behavior changed:
- `src/ui.rs::TerminalUi` tracks visual selections for the focused pane with character, line, and block modes.
- Command-mode `v`, `V`, and `Ctrl-V` start character, line, and block selection.
- `h/j/k/l` and arrow keys move the visual selection head.
- `y` copies the active visual selection; `Y` copies linewise.
- Copied text is exposed through `TerminalUi::take_copied_text()` and emitted to terminals with an OSC 52 clipboard escape.
- Visual selections render as reversed cells in the selected pane.
- `src/ui_harness.rs::UiHarness` and `src/terminal_app.rs::TerminalAppCore` route keys through `TerminalUi::handle_key_with_content(...)` so selection can operate on current rendered pane text.

Tests added in `tests/ui_harness.rs`:
- `scripted_harness_visual_line_yanks_left_pane_line`
- `scripted_harness_visual_character_yanks_right_pane_text`
- `scripted_harness_visual_block_yanks_left_pane_rectangle`

Verification:
- Ran focused check: `cargo test --test ui_harness`
- Result: failed with 31 passed, 1 failed.
- All three new visual-mode tests passed.
- Exact blocker: unrelated existing/other-feature behavior failed in `scripted_harness_hides_closed_chat_and_reopens_it` at `tests/ui_harness.rs:46`, asserting hidden `user-1` should not appear in the left pane.

Patch summary for `user-2` / `b30becd4`:

The patch adds strict selected-agent slash command routing. In [src/workspace.rs](src/workspace.rs), `WorkLeafController::send_message` detects messages that trim to `/` plus a non-whitespace token, such as `/status` or `/fork`, before the ordinary busy/send path. Those commands are appended to the selected agent chat as `user: ...`, executed through a backend-command worker, and the backend command output is appended back to that same chat.

The backend path is provider-neutral: [src/agent_runtime.rs](src/agent_runtime.rs) extends `AgentBackend` with default `run_command` / `run_command_streaming` methods, [src/cli.rs](src/cli.rs) routes strict slash messages through `CommandChat::run_agent_backend_command_streaming_with_ids`, and [src/codex.rs](src/codex.rs) implements the command path by building a raw Codex resume invocation through `CodexBackend::build_command_invocation`.

Coverage added in [tests/workspace.rs](tests/workspace.rs) proves:
- `/status` uses the backend command path.
- `/fork` uses the backend command path.
- `/status` is not received by the normal backend `send` path.
- `/ status` is not treated as a slash command because the command token is whitespace-prefixed.

Verification run in this session:
- `cargo test --test workspace`
- Result: passed, `19 passed; 0 failed`

Broad checks were not run in this patch-agent step because the orchestrator instructed running at most one focused validation after applying the provisional patch. No real-agent smoke scenario was run, and no exact local blocker was captured for that missing real-agent verification.

reviewer findings:
I’m reading the orchestrator-provided bundle, then I’ll check the changed behavior against the architecture and tests before returning only the review verdict.

The bundle confirms this is an agent-facing change: `WorkLeafController::send_message` and `CommandChat` are in the path from selected terminal chat input to provider backend execution, so real-agent verification is in scope.

I’m checking for behavior bugs in the command path itself, not just the stated verification gap. The key question is whether `/status` reaches the same Codex session safely and preserves lifecycle controls.

I found one concrete behavioral risk beyond verification: the new Codex slash-command process is launched without being associated with the target agent id, which matters for per-agent interrupt handling. I’m pinning that to exact file lines from the bundle now.

The review evidence is sufficient: the patch path is clear from `workspace.rs` to `cli.rs` to `codex.rs`, and the launch/interrupt tracking mismatch is directly visible in the new command implementation.

FINDINGS

- `src/codex.rs:465` launches slash-command resumes with `agent_id: None`, so the process is not recorded in `active_processes` by `run_invocation_streaming` (`src/codex.rs:282-287`). The selected-agent path is `WorkLeafController::send_message` (`src/workspace.rs:259-275`) -> `CommandChat::run_agent_backend_command_streaming_with_ids` (`src/cli.rs:479-490`) -> `CodexBackend::run_command_streaming`; `CodexBackend::interrupt` only terminates PIDs found in `active_processes` (`src/codex.rs:399-409`). Result: a long-running or stuck `/status`/`/fork` command cannot be interrupted per-agent even though the UI reports Ctrl-C was sent. Pass `Some(agent_id)` into `run_invocation_streaming` for command execution and add coverage for interrupting an in-flight backend command.

- Required real-agent verification is missing for this agent-facing behavior. The patch changes selected-agent slash command routing and Codex resume invocation behavior (`src/workspace.rs:259-290`, `src/cli.rs:450-493`, `src/codex.rs:458-497`), but the review scope says: “No real-agent smoke scenario was run, and no exact local blocker was captured.” Evidence that would resolve this is an exact real Codex scenario, for example launching a real user agent, sending `/status` through selected-agent chat, and reporting the visible result, or the exact pre-agent initialization/auth blocker plus the automated tests that cover the behavior.

Patch `7fb48f9` adds reviewed-feature closure flow for `user-3`.

Behavior change:
- `src/workspace.rs::WorkLeafController` tracks reviewed patch agents with `pending_feature_done_decisions` and `closed_features`.
- When `CommandChatResult::ReviewComplete` is resolved, `apply_agent_result` calls `prompt_feature_done_decision`, appending `work-leaf: is this feature done? yes/no` to the reviewed `user-*` patch agent chat.
- If the user replies `yes` or `y`, `send_message` handles it locally through `close_feature_if_confirmed`, appends `work-leaf: feature closed`, and does not send that confirmation to the backend.
- If the user types another message after a pending or closed state, `reopen_feature_if_needed` appends `work-leaf: feature reopened`, then normal agent send resumes.

Terminal/UI behavior:
- `src/ui.rs::TerminalUi::set_agent_hidden_state` supports hiding/revealing an agent row.
- `src/terminal_app.rs::TerminalAppCore` maps the controller chat notices to UI state: done prompt keeps the patch chat highlighted, close hides it, reopen shows it again.
- `src/ui_harness.rs::UiHarness` exposes close/reopen helpers for automatic UI tests.

Tests added/updated:
- `tests/workspace.rs` verifies the review-done prompt appears after automatic review, `yes` closes locally without backend send, and later typing reopens and resumes backend chat.
- `tests/ui_harness.rs` verifies closed chats disappear from the left pane and reopen into the list.

Verification run:
- `cargo test --test workspace review`
- Result: passed, 10 passed, 0 failed.

Not run:
- Broad required checks (`cargo fmt`, full clippy, full test suite) were not run in this patch-agent step.
- No real-agent smoke scenario was run. This patch affects terminal/controller agent chat behavior, so real-agent verification remains missing unless performed by a later review/linearize step.
